already_supported: concept identity as family basis plus local offset
attribute directions as local chart axes
current_puzzle.main_open_point: shared object manifold to discriminative geometry compatibility

feature_to_code_process: multimodal input first lands on a family patch of the object atlas
local evidence forms a concept-specific family offset inside that patch
attribute-like directions refine the concept state on the same local chart
admissible-update constraints decide which updates are allowed
restricted overlaps decide whether state can be transported toward memory, relation, or readout charts
bridge-role lift organizes object entries into relation and role structure
brain-side realizations should be region-parameterized projections of this same inventory-conditioned system

Q1_encoding_object_layer.inventory_support: concept entries + family patch + family offsets
Q1_encoding_object_layer.why: inventory already reconstructs object kernel, family basis, local neighbors, and cross-family margins

Q2_local_update_law.inventory_support: inventory provides stable concept offsets and family-specific low-rank axes
Q2_local_update_law.why: safe update directions are not fully solved, but inventory now gives the local geometry updates must preserve

Q3_write_read_separation.inventory_support: inventory exposes which concept-local coordinates remain stable under family-centered representation
Q3_write_read_separation.why: it constrains what must be preserved, though write/read dynamics themselves still need direct stress tests

Q4_bridge_role_kernel.inventory_support: relation templates can now be layered onto concept entries and attribute axes
Q4_bridge_role_kernel.why: bridge-role structure is not fully reconstructed, but the atlas now offers the object substrate they must lift from

Q5_crossmodal_consistency.inventory_support: concept entries already unify visual, tactile, and language-like state into one atlas slot
Q5_crossmodal_consistency.why: inventory directly supports the shared object manifold hypothesis

Q6_discriminative_geometry.inventory_support: cross-family margins and restricted overlaps sharply constrain readout geometry
Q6_discriminative_geometry.why: inventory now explains why direct object-to-disc collapse fails and why restricted overlap readout is needed

Q7_brain_mapping_3d.inventory_support: atlas entries can become natural probes for cortical mapping and falsification
Q7_brain_mapping_3d.why: the abstract probe set is ready, but brain-side execution is still pending

unified_system.inventory_entry: E_c = (f_c, z_c, delta_c, N_same(c), N_cross(c), A_c, R_c, S_c)
unified_system.admissibility: A(I) = INTERSECT_f [K_ret^(f)(I) INTERSECT K_id^(f)(I) INTERSECT K_read^(f)(I) INTERSECT K_bridge^(f)(I)] INTERSECT K_phase
unified_system.viability: M_feas(I) = UNION_f [U_object^(f)(I) UNION U_memory^(f)(I) UNION U_disc^(f)(I) UNION U_relation^(f)(I)] UNION U_phase
unified_system.joint_constraint: Delta_t in A(I) and trajectory(z_t) subset of M_feas(I)

bridge_role_status.bridge_lift: rho_c = G_rel(E_c) = G_rel(f_c, z_c, delta_c, A_c, S_c)
bridge_role_status.bridge_admissibility: bridge lift is valid only if sigma_rel(c) stays above the minimum relation threshold
bridge_role_status.family_anchor_condition: relation lift must remain inside the family-conditioned object-relation overlap band

verdict.core_answer: The current best synthesis is that brain encoding is a controlled encoding dynamical system organized around an inventory-conditioned object atlas.
verdict.next_theory_target: close stress-to-readout transport and brain-side execution so the remaining open points move from partial to strong support
